summary: The author secured 1st place in BirdCLEF 2025 by implementing a multi-iterative noisy student self-training pipeline on 20-second audio chunks. Key innovations include mixing pseudo-labeled soundscapes with labeled data using a constant 0.5 blending weight, applying a power transform to pseudo-labels to mitigate noise accumulation across iterations, and sampling pseudo-labeled data with a WeightedRandomSampler based on label probability sums. A dedicated model for underrepresented Amphibia and Insecta groups using extended Xeno-Canto data, combined with overlapping framewise prediction averaging during inference, drove the final score.
extensive_summary:
## Data & Preprocessing
The solution processes 20-second audio chunks normalized by absmax. These chunks are converted to mel spectrograms using a sample rate of 32kHz, 224 mel bins, frequency range 0-16kHz, n_fft of 4096, hop size of 1252, and top_db of 80.0. The spectrograms are 0-1 normalized and repeated 3 times to form the input channels. External Xeno-Canto data was filtered for durations under 60 seconds and used to train a dedicated model for the Amphibia and Insecta groups.

## Model Architecture & Supervised Training
The core architecture combines a Self-Event Detection (SED) head with Gem frequency pooling. Backbones evolved across training stages, starting with `tf_efficientnet_b0.ns_jft_in1k` and `regnety_008.pycls_in1k`, then progressing to `tf_efficientnet_b3/ns_jft_in1k`, `regnety_016.tv2_in1k`, `tf_efficientnet_b4.ns_jft_in1k`, and `eca_nfnet_l0.ra2_in1k`. The supervised stage used CrossEntropy loss, AdamW optimizer, CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts scheduler, and MixUp augmentation (p=0.5) on raw audio with left-side zero padding to ensure consistent mixing.

## Multi-Iterative Noisy Student Self-Training
After hitting a supervised ceiling, the author pseudo-labeled unlabeled soundscapes and integrated them via MixUp. A constant blending weight of 0.5 (Beta parameters = inf) proved superior to Beta-distributed weights. Stochastic Depth (`drop_path_rate = 0.15`) was applied exclusively during self-training to inject noise and force robust feature learning. To combat noise amplification in later iterations, a power transform greater than 1 was applied to pseudo-label probabilities, preserving confident signals while suppressing noise. A WeightedRandomSampler prioritized soundscapes with higher sums of maximum label probabilities, stabilizing training.

## Inference & Ensembling
Inference leverages overlapping framewise predictions from neighboring chunks, averaged to act as a 1D sliding-window segmentation and implicit TTA. Post-processing includes edge padding, smoothing with weights [0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.2, 0.1], and delta shift TTA. The final submission ensembles 7 models from different stages and backbones with equal weights, optimized via OpenVINO for inference speed.
competition_flow: Raw audio is split into 20-second chunks, converted to mel spectrograms, and fed into CNNs with an SED head; models are trained via supervised learning and multi-iterative noisy student self-training with MixUp and power-transformed pseudo-labels, then ensembled with overlapping framewise averaging and delta shift TTA for final predictions.
data_reading: 20-second audio chunks normalized by absmax, converted to mel spectrograms (32kHz, 224 mel bins, 0-16kHz, n_fft=4096, hop=1252, top_db=80) and repeated 3 times as input channels
data_processing: Mel spectrogram conversion with specified parameters
MixUp augmentation (p=0.5) on raw audio with left-side zero padding
Power transform on pseudo-labels to reduce noise
WeightedRandomSampler based on sum of max label probabilities
Smoothing and delta shift TTA during inference
models: SED head with Gem frequency pooling
cv_strategy: 5 folds with at least 1 sample per label per fold; validated primarily on Public LB due to poor CV/LB correlation.
ensembling: Final submission used equal weights across 7 models from different training stages and backbones, combined with overlapping framewise prediction averaging, smoothing, and delta shift TTA.
insights: Longer 20-second input chunks optimally balance model performance and inference speed compared to shorter durations.
MixUp with a constant 0.5 blending weight prevents suppression of meaningful signals when mixing clean and noisy pseudo-labeled data.
Applying a power transform greater than 1 to pseudo-label probabilities effectively mitigates noise accumulation in multi-iterative self-training.
WeightedRandomSampler based on the sum of maximum label probabilities stabilizes training by prioritizing higher-quality pseudo-labeled samples.
critical_findings: Family-level Insecta labels from Xeno-Canto worsened results when used as-is, but assigning unique species-level labels improved performance.
CrossEntropy outperformed BCE/Focal losses because it penalizes overrepresented negative classes more strongly when rare positives are misclassified.
Stochastic Depth only improved results during self-training, not supervised training, confirming the noisy student dynamic.
what_did_not_work: Concatenating pseudo-labeled data into training batches separately failed to improve results.
Using Beta distribution parameters too low for MixUp blending weights suppressed meaningful signals.
Training deeper models for the dedicated Amphibia/Insecta group dropped scores.
Raising the minimum samples per species to 5 for the dedicated model dropped scores.

papers.title: Self-training with Noisy Student improves ImageNet classification
Design Choices for Enhancing Noisy Student Self-Training
